3
Item 1
ITEM 1. BUSINESS
GENERAL
Embracing Our Future
Microsoft is a technology company whose mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. We strive to create local opportunity, growth, and impact in every country around the world. We are creating the platforms and tools, powered by artificial intelligence ("AI"), that deliver better, faster, and more effective solutions to support small and large business competitiveness, improve educational and health outcomes, grow public-sector efficiency,  and empower human ingenuity. From infrastructure and data, to business applications and collaboration, we provide unique, differentiated value to customers.
In a world of increasing economic complexity, AI has the power to revolutionize many types of work. Microsoft is now innovating and expanding our portfolio with AI capabilities to help people and organizations overcome today's challenges and emerge stronger. Customers 
…[truncated 19275 chars]…
ing, and inclusive work environment that enables our global employees to create products and services that further our mission.
8
Item 1
As of June 30, 2023, we employed approximately 221,000 people on a full-time basis, 120,000 in the U.S. and 101,000 internationally. Of the total employed people, 89,000 were in operations, including manufacturing, distribution, product support, and consulting services; 72,000 were in product research and development; 45,000 were in sales and marketing; and 15,000 were in general and administration. Certain employees are subject to collective bargaining agreements.
Our Culture
Microsoft's culture is grounded in growth mindset. This means everyone is on a continuous journey to learn and grow, operating as one company instead of multiple siloed businesses.
Our employee listening systems enable us to gather feedback directly from our workforce to inform our programs and employee needs globally. Employees participate in our Employee Signals surveys, which cover a variety of topics such as thriving, inclusion, team culture, wellbeing, and learning and development. We also collect Daily Signals employee survey responses, giving us real-time insights into ways we can support our employees. In addition to Employee Signals and Daily Signals surveys, we gain insights through onboarding, exit surveys, internal Viva Engage channels, employee Q&A sessions, and our internal AskHR Service support.
Diversity and inclusion are core to our business model, and we hold ourselves accountable for driving global systemic change in our workforce and creating an inclusive work environment. We support multiple highly active Employee Resource Groups for women, families, racial and ethnic minorities, military, people with disabilities, and employees who identify as LGBTQIA+, where employees can go for support, networking, and community-building. As described in our 2022 Proxy Statement, annual performance and compensation reviews of our senior leadership team include an evaluation of their contributions to employee culture and diversity. To ensure accountability over time, we publicly disclose our progress on a multitude of workforce metrics including:
- Detailed breakdowns of gender, racial, and ethnic minority representation in our employee population, with data by job types, levels, and segments of our business.
- Our EEO-1 report (equal employment opportunity).
- Disability representation.
- Pay equity (see details below).
Total Rewards and Pay Equity
We develop dynamic, sustainable, market-driven, and strategic programs with the goal of providing a highly differentiated portfolio to attract, reward, and retain top talent and enable our employees to thrive. These programs reinforce our culture and values such as collaboration and growth mindset. Managers evaluate and recommend rewards based on, for example, how well we leverage the work of others and contribute to the success of our colleagues. We monitor pay equity and career progress across multiple dimensions. Our total compensation opportunity is highly differentiated and is market competitive.
In order to manage our costs in a dynamic, competitive environment, in fiscal year 2023 we announced that base salaries of salaried employees would remain at fiscal year 2022 levels. Pay increases continue to be available for rewards-eligible hourly and equivalent employees. We will continue our practice of investing in stock for all rewards-eligible employees, salaried and hourly, and investing in bonuses for all eligible employees.
9
Item 1
Since 2016, we have reported on pay equity as part of our annual Diversity and Inclusion report. In 2022, we reported that all racial and ethnic minority employees in the U.S. combined earn $1.008 for every $1.000 earned by their white counterparts, that women in the U.S. earn $1.007 for every $1.000 earned by their counterparts who are men, and that women outside the U.S. earn $1.002 for every $1.000 earned by their counterparts outside the U.S. who are men. In this year's report, we again expanded our pay equity data beyond the U.S. to report on 61 additional countries (up from 12 last year), representing 99.8% of our global Microsoft workforce.
In addition, we began reporting on unadjusted median pay in our annual report, comparing total pay amounts for all employees regardless of factors such as job title, level, or tenure. For employees who are eligible for rewards, the analysis showed that total pay for women is 89.6% of total pay for men in the U.S. and 86.2% outside of the U.S., and total pay for racial and ethnic minorities in the U.S. is 89.9% of total pay for white employees. As we continue to increase representation for women and racial and ethnic minorities at more senior levels, and continue to ensure pay equity for all, the gap between the medians will reduce.
Our intended result is a global performance and development approach that fosters our culture, and competitive compensation that ensures equitable pay by role while supporting pay for performance.
Wellbeing and Hybrid Work
Microsoft is committed to supporting our employees' wellbeing while they are at work and in their personal lives. We have invested significantly in wellbeing, and offer a differentiated benefits package which includes many physical, emotional, and financial wellness programs including counseling through the Microsoft CARES Employee Assistance Program, mental wellbeing support, flexible fitness benefits, disability accommodations, savings and investment tools, adoption assistance, and back-up care for children and elders. Finally, our Occupational Health and Safety program helps ensure employees can stay safe while they are working.
We introduced Hybrid Workplace Flexibility Guidance to better support leaders, managers, and employees in hybrid work scenarios. Our ongoing survey data shows that 93% of employees value the flexibility related to work location, work site, and work hours, and 78% are satisfied with the quality of connection with co-workers. There is no one-size-fits-all approach to flexible work at Microsoft. As a company, we will continue to leverage data and research to inform decision making, balancing the needs of business, team, and individual.
Learning and Development
We offer a range of learning opportunities, including personalized opportunities on our internal and external learning portals, in-classroom learning, required learning on compliance and company culture, on-the-job advancement opportunities, and manager coaching. We also provide customized manager learning, new employee orientation, and tools for operating in a flexible hybrid work environment.
All Microsoft employees globally access our single Viva Learning tool for both required and personal choice learning. This includes courses focused on our core principles and compliance matters, such as Business Conduct, Privacy, Security Foundations, and Harassment Prevention. We also deliver skills training for employees based on their profession and role discipline.
We have over 27,000 people managers, all of whom must complete between 20-33 hours of compulsory training on leadership and management and are assigned additional targeted training on an ongoing basis related to people management, compliance, and culture.

We operate our business and report our financial performance using three segments: Productivity and Business Processes, Intelligent Cloud, and More Personal Computing. Our segments provide management with a comprehensive financial view of our key businesses. The segments enable the alignment of strategies and objectives across the development, sales, marketing, and services organizations, and they provide a framework for timely and rational allocation of resources within businesses.

**Quantitative Report: MSFT as of 2024-04-01**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
*   **Revenue & Earnings:** Latest quarter (2023-12-31) revenue was $62.02B, up 17.6% YoY and 9.7% QoQ. TTM revenue is $227.58B. Quarterly EPS diluted grew 33.2% YoY to $2.93 but decreased 2.0% QoQ from the prior quarter's $2.99. TTM net income is $82.54B.
*   **Margins:** Gross margin for the latest quarter was 69.7% (gross profit $42.40B / revenue $62.02B). Operating margin was 44.2% (operating income $27.03B). Net margin (trailing) was 36.3%.
*   **Cash-Flow:** Quarterly cash flow data is unavailable for all supplied periods.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Total assets grew to $470.56B from $344.61B over the 8-quarter lookback. Total equity grew to $238.27B from $162.92B over the same period. Latest quarter cash was $81.02B, down from $143.95B in the prior quarter.

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** High-quality margins (gross 69.7%, operating 44.2%, net 36.3%). YoY earnings growth (+33.2%) is strong and outpaces revenue growth (+17.6%), indicating positive operating leverage. However, QoQ net income and EPS dipped (-1.9% and -2.0%) despite a +9.7% QoQ revenue increase, which may indicate period-specific cost factors. Detailed cash flow data is unavailable to assess earnings conversion.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Strong net cash position: cash ($81.02B) exceeds total debt ($47.18B) by $33.84B. Debt-to-equity is low at 0.198. Total equity has increased 46.3% over the 8-quarter period, strengthening the base.

**3. Valuation Versus Supplied History**
*   **Multiples:** Latest PE (diluted) is 38.065. This is at the top of its 365-day range of 29.8 to 39.7. EV/EBIT is 30.916, also at the high end of its 23.6 to 31.6 range. Price-to-Book is 13.187, within its 10.6 to 13.9 range.
*   **Trajectory:** The PE multiple has expanded significantly from 31.824 on 2023-04-03 to 38.065. Earnings yield is 2.6%, near its historical low for the period.

**4. Price Level and Return Context**
*   **Price Level:** The stock closed at $420.72 on 2024-03-28. The 52-week high is $430.82 and the low is $275.37.
*   **Returns:** The quarterly return (prior ~63 trading days) was +12.5% (from $374.07 to $420.72). The annual return (prior ~252 trading days) was +52.9% (from $275.23 to $420.72).
*   **Context:** The stock's price appreciation has significantly outpaced its TTM EPS growth trajectory, reflecting the observed multiple expansion.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**
*   **Upside:** Continued strong YoY revenue growth (+17.6%) and operating leverage (net income +33.2% YoY). A fortress balance sheet (net cash $33.84B) provides resilience.
*   **Downside:** Valuation is extended; the PE (38.1x) and EV/EBIT (30.9x) are at or near the top of their one-year ranges. The QoQ EPS decline (-2.0%) contrasts with revenue growth, warranting monitoring. The absence of cash flow data is a gap in the evidence.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The quantitative evidence shows a company with strong top-line growth, high and stable margins, and a pristine balance sheet. However, the stock is priced for near-flawless execution: its valuation multiples (PE 38.1x, EV/EBIT 30.9x) are at the ceiling of their recent history, and the 53% annual price return has far outstripped the 33% annual EPS growth. The risk is that continued multiple expansion is required to justify the current price given the elevated starting point. The reward hinges on the durability of the strong fundamental growth to either support or expand the premium valuation. The lack of supplied cash flow data limits a full assessment of earnings quality.
MSFT.qualitative_report:
## MSFT — Qualitative Franchise & Risk Report
**Decision Date: 2024-04-01 | Evidence Window: 2023-07-27 to 2024-03-31**

---

### 1. Business / Franchise Description

**Source: 10-K filed 2023-07-27**

Microsoft operates across three reporting segments — **Productivity and Business Processes** (Office 365, Dynamics 365, LinkedIn, Microsoft Teams), **Intelligent Cloud** (Azure, server products, Nuance), and **More Personal Computing** (Windows, Surface, Xbox, Search/Advertising). The company reported FY2023 revenue exceeding $211 billion (web context, 2024-03-20) and employed ~221,000 people as of June 30, 2023.

The franchise is anchored by **deep enterprise lock-in** via Microsoft 365, Azure's hybrid cloud infrastructure, and an integrated security/compliance stack. Management's stated strategic ambition is to infuse **generative AI across the entire product portfolio** — Microsoft 365 Copilot, Azure AI, GitHub Copilot, Bing Chat, and gaming — positioning the company as a platform-layer AI beneficiary. A **long-term exclusive partnership with OpenAI** (Azure powers all OpenAI workloads; Microsoft deploys OpenAI models across consumer and enterprise products) is the centerpiece of the AI strategy. The Activision Blizzard acquisition (announced January 2022) extends the gaming franchise across subscription (Game Pass), console, PC, and mobile.

---

### 2. Recent Narrative Developments (Dated)

| Date | Development | Category |
|---|---|---|
| **2024-03-31** | Multiple analyst/article pieces flag MSFT trailing P/E at a **near-20-year high** — valuation concern surfaced. | Valuation / Sentiment |
| **2024-03-31** | Cathie Wood cited Palantir as a potential **disruptor to Microsoft in AI** (web news). | Competition |
| **2024-03-31** | Articles frame Nvidia as potentially **surpassing MSFT** as most valuable Magnificent Seven stock. | Competitive positioning |
| **2024-03-30** | Intel, Qualcomm, Arm, Google, and others forming a **new AI alliance** — potential competitive counterweight in AI infrastructure. | Competition |
| **2024-03-25** | Seattle Times analysis drawing parallels between **current antitrust risks** for big tech and Microsoft's historic litigation era. | Regulation |
| **2024-03-24** | Motley Fool article reaffirming MSFT's **early AI integration advantage** across broad product cross-section. | AI positioning (positive) |
| **2024-03-20** | Market analysis noting $211B+ FY2023 revenue and **diversified, resilient revenue streams**. | Franchise strength |
| **2024-03-11** | European Data Protection Supervisor ruled **EU Commission's use of Microsoft 365 infringes data protection rules**; corrective measures due by December 9, 2024. | Regulation |
| **2024-03-09** | Academic/business analysis: OpenAI partnership expected to **significantly boost Azure Cloud growth** through AI innovation pipeline. | AI catalyst |
| **2024-03-06** | Microsoft employee sent letter to **FTC warning AI tools could generate harmful/offensive images**, including sexualized content. | AI safety / Regulatory |
| **2024-02-27** | Reuters: Microsoft's **deal with Mistral AI faces EU regulatory scrutiny**; investment structured as equity conversion in next funding round. | Regulation |
| **2024-02-27** | Washington Post reference to **historic antitrust ruling** (contextual risk narrative). | Regulation |

---

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks & Offsets

**Risks:**

- **Regulatory / Antitrust Overhang (confirmed):** EU scrutiny of the Mistral AI investment (2024-02-27), data protection findings against EU Commission's use of Microsoft 365 (2024-03-11), and renewed antitrust narrative in U.S. media (2024-03-25) collectively signal an escalating multi-jurisdictional regulatory environment. The 10-K itself devotes substantial space to regulatory risk disclosures.

- **AI Safety / Responsible AI Concerns (confirmed):** A Microsoft employee escalated concerns to the FTC about AI-generated harmful content (2024-03-06). This could invite regulatory action or damage the company's "responsible AI" positioning that management explicitly emphasizes in the 10-K.

- **Valuation Stretch (confirmed):** Multiple sources on 2024-03-31 note the stock is at a near-20-year high on a trailing P/E basis, which may limit upside and amplify downside on any fundamental disappointment.

- **Competitive AI Ecosystem Development (confirmed):** A new AI infrastructure alliance including Google, Intel, Qualcomm, and Arm (2024-03-30) and narrative around Palantir as an AI disruptor (2024-03-31) suggest Microsoft's early AI lead is not uncontested.

**Offsets:**

- **Deep AI Integration Across Product Suite (confirmed):** Microsoft has embedded generative AI into Microsoft 365 Copilot, Azure AI, GitHub Copilot, Bing, and gaming. The 10-K describes AI as spanning "the entire company." This breadth of deployment is a structural moat versus narrower competitors.

- **Exclusive OpenAI Cloud Partnership (confirmed, 10-K):** Azure is OpenAI's exclusive cloud provider, creating a mutually reinforcing flywheel between model development and infrastructure demand.

- **Diversified Revenue & Scale (confirmed):** $211B+ revenue across productivity, cloud, and consumer segments provides resilience against any single market dislocation.

- **Enterprise Lock-In (inferred from 10-K):** The integrated Microsoft 365 + Azure + security stack creates high switching costs for enterprise customers, sustaining the cloud and productivity franchises.

---

### 4. Qualitative Assessment

**Positive, with material caveats.**

Microsoft's franchise strength — diversified revenue, enterprise lock-in, and a first-mover position in enterprise AI via the OpenAI partnership — is clearly intact as of early 2024. The AI monetization narrative is supported by confirmed product integration across the portfolio and growing Azure demand. However, the qualitative picture is tempered by (a) a valuation at multi-decade highs on trailing earnings, (b) intensifying multi-jurisdictional regulatory risk around AI and data practices, and (c) emerging competitive alliances that could narrow Microsoft's AI infrastructure advantage over time. The confirmed evidence supports a positive franchise read, but the risk/reward balance is more nuanced than the uniformly bullish AI narrative in the recent press suggests.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Microsoft's franchise is exceptionally strong—17.6% YoY revenue growth, 44.2% operating margins, and a fortress balance sheet with $33.84B net cash—but the stock is priced for perfection at a near-20-year high P/E of 38.1x, leaving little margin of safety. The risk-adjusted reward is mildly positive given durable AI-driven growth catalysts, but the elevated valuation and emerging regulatory headwinds cap upside conviction.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Microsoft's franchise is exceptionally strong—17.6% YoY revenue growth, 44.2% operating margins, and a fortress balance sheet with $33.84B net cash—but the stock is priced for perfection at a near-20-year high P/E of 38.1x, leaving little margin of safety. The risk-adjusted reward is mildly positive given durable AI-driven growth catalysts, but the elevated valuation and emerging regulatory headwinds cap upside conviction.
key_factors: Valuation at ceiling: PE of 38.1x and EV/EBIT of 30.9x are at or near the top of their one-year ranges (29.8–39.7 and 23.6–31.6 respectively), with the stock's 52.9% annual return far outpacing 33.2% EPS growth, requiring continued multiple expansion to justify current prices.

Strong fundamental momentum: Q4 2023 revenue of $62.02B grew 17.6% YoY with 69.7% gross margin and 44.2% operating margin, while net income grew 33.2% YoY demonstrating operating leverage; balance sheet carries $33.84B net cash with debt-to-equity of just 0.198.

AI monetization catalyst: Exclusive OpenAI cloud partnership and broad AI integration across Microsoft 365 Copilot, Azure AI, and GitHub Copilot position MSFT as the primary enterprise AI platform beneficiary, supporting durable revenue growth.

Regulatory and competitive headwinds: EU scrutiny of the Mistral AI deal (2024-02-27), data protection ruling against EU Commission's use of Microsoft 365 (2024-03-11), and a new AI infrastructure alliance including Google, Intel, Qualcomm, and Arm (2024-03-30) represent material risks to the premium valuation.

ITEM 1.    Business
OVERVIEW
Visa is one of the world's leaders in digital payments. Our purpose is to uplift everyone, everywhere by being the best way to pay and be paid. We facilitate global commerce and money movement across more than 200 countries and territories among a global set of consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities through innovative technologies.
Since Visa's early days in 1958, we have been in the business of facilitating payments between consumers and businesses. We are focused on extending, enhancing and investing in our proprietary advanced transaction processing network, VisaNet, to offer a single connection point for facilitating payment transactions to multiple endpoints through various form factors. As a network of networks enabling global movement of money through all available networks, we are working to provide payment solutions and services for everyone, everywhere. Through our network, we offer products, solutions and services that facilitate secure, reliable and efficient money movement for participants in the ecosystem.
- We facilitate secure, reliable and efficient money movement among consumers, issuing and acquiring financial institutions and merchants. We have traditionally referred to this structure as the "four-party" model. Please see Our Core Business discussion below. As the payments ecosystem continues to evolve, we have broadened this model to include digital banks, digital wallets and a range of financial
…[truncated 20518 chars]…
anager, Visa Consumer Authentication Service, and payment-decisioning solutions from CardinalCommerce empower financial institutions and merchants with tools that help automate and simplify fraud prevention and enhance payment security.
Aligned to our network of networks strategy, Visa is increasingly bringing our expertise and capabilities to emerging fraud challenges, working with network operators and financial institutions to help mitigate fraud. These value-added fraud prevention tools layer on top of a suite of our network programs that protect the safety and integrity of the payment ecosystem, and along with our investments in intelligence and technology, help to prevent, detect and mitigate threats. These programs and Visa's fraud prevention expertise are among the core benefits of

being part of the Visa network. Through the combined efforts of security and identity tools and services, payment and cyber intelligence, insights and learnings from client or partner breach investigations, and law enforcement engagement, Visa helps protect financial institutions and merchants from fraud and solve payment security challenges.
Open Banking
In March 2022, Visa acquired Tink AB, an open banking platform, to catalyze fintech innovation and accelerate the development and adoption of open banking securely and at scale. Visa's open banking capabilities range from data access use cases, such as account verification, balance check and personal finance management, to payment initiation capabilities, such as account-to-account transactions and merchant payments. These capabilities can help our partner businesses deliver valuable services to their customers.
Advisory Services
Visa Consulting and Analytics (VCA) is the payments consulting advisory arm of Visa. The combination of our deep payments expertise, proprietary analytical models applied to a breadth of data and our economic intelligence allows us to identify actionable insights, make recommendations and help implement solutions that can drive better business decisions and measurable outcomes for clients. VCA offers consulting services for issuers, acquirers, merchants, fintechs and other partners, spanning the entire customer journey from acquisition to retention. Further, VCA Managed Services, our dedicated execution arm within the consulting division, is being increasingly utilized by clients to implement our recommendations and wider value added services product enablement.

We are fortifying the key foundations of our business model, which consist of becoming a network of networks, our technology platforms, security, brand and talent.

Network of Networks
Our network of networks strategy means moving money to all endpoints and to all form factors, using all available networks and being a single connection point for our partners; and providing our value added services on all transactions, no matter the network. The key component of our network of networks strategy is interoperability. We are opening up our network and increasingly using other networks to reach accounts we could not otherwise reach and enabling new types of money movement. Visa B2B Connect, Visa Direct, and Visa+ are examples of our strategy.
Technology Platforms
Visa's leading technology platforms comprise software, hardware, data centers and a large telecommunications infrastructure. Visa's four data centers are a critical part of our global processing environment and have a high redundancy of network connectivity, power and cooling designed to provide continuous availability of systems. Together, these systems deliver the secure, convenient and reliable service that our clients and consumers expect from the Visa brand.
Security
Our in-depth, multi-layer security approach includes a formal program to devalue sensitive and/or personal data through various cryptographic means; embedded security in the software development lifecycle; identity and access management controls to protect against unauthorized access; and advanced cyber detection and response capabilities. We deploy security tools that help keep our clients and consumers safe. We also invest significantly in our comprehensive approach to cybersecurity. We deploy security technologies to protect data confidentiality, the integrity of our network and service availability to strengthen our core cybersecurity capabilities to minimize risk. Our

payments fraud disruption team continually monitors threats to the payments ecosystem to help ensure attacks are detected and prevented efficiently and effectively.
Brand
Visa's strong brand helps deliver added value to our clients and their customers, financial institutions, merchants and partners through compelling brand expressions, a wide range of products and services as well as innovative brand and marketing efforts. In line with our commitment to an expansive and diverse range of partnerships for the benefit of our stakeholders, Visa is a sponsor of top entertainment and sports events including the FIFA Women's World Cup 2023TM, the Olympic and Paralympic Games, and the Super Bowl.
Talent
Attracting, developing and advancing the best talent globally is critical to our continued success. This year we grew our total workforce from approximately 26,500 in fiscal year 2022 to approximately 28,800 employees in fiscal year 2023, an increase of 9 percent year over year. Voluntary workforce turnover (rolling 12-month attrition) was 6 percent as of September 30, 2023. Visa employees are located in more than 80 countries and territories, with 55 percent located outside the U.S. At the end of fiscal year 2023, Visa's global workforce was 58 percent men and 42 percent women, and women represented 36 percent of Visa's leadership (defined as vice president level and above). In the U.S., ethnicity of our workforce was 42 percent Asian, 8 percent Black, 13 percent Hispanic, 3 percent Other and 35 percent White. For our U.S. leadership, the breakdown was 18 percent Asian, 6 percent Black, 13 percent Hispanic, 3 percent Other and 60 percent White.
Given Visa's ambitious growth agenda and efforts to achieve our purpose, we have focused on enhancing our employees' expertise across our business. This includes an enhanced development program for our senior leaders and a formal technology apprenticeship program to help us broaden and strengthen our talent channels and pipelines. We have also committed to providing employees with the tools they need to do their work more quickly and easily, including an artificial intelligence or AI-driven portal with a searchable knowledge base to create customized results and bespoke solutions. We enhanced our mental well-being and retirement benefits, which is reflective of our key priority to take care of our employees.
We also are dedicated to ensuring that employees feel valued in their day-to-day work. During our global employee engagement survey last year, we learned that our employees wanted more opportunities to recognize and be recognized, in more informal ways. In response, Visa developed a program that better enabled employees to provide peer-to-peer recognition for each other's contributions. Using UPLIFT, Visa's new recognition platform, employees can celebrate their peers' achievements, send e-cards to celebrate the employee journey (from welcoming new hires to recognizing service anniversaries), use an automated internal networking tool that matches employees based on smart algorithms, and more. Importantly, all our recognition categories are grounded in behaviors that reflect our employee value proposition or Visa's Leadership Principles – further reinforcing that at Visa, it is not only about what you achieve, but how you do it. Employee engagement in peer recognition has significantly increased since the launch, with monthly active users reaching 78 percent in September 2023, compared to 45 percent in September 2022

**V Quantitative Report (Decision Date: 2024-04-01)**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
- **Revenue**: Demonstrated consistent quarterly growth over 8 quarters, from 7.19B (2022-03-31) to 8.63B (2023-12-31). Latest YoY growth of 8.8%. TTM revenue: 33.35B.
- **Earnings**: Net income followed a similar upward trajectory from 3.65B to 4.89B. Latest YoY net income growth: 17.0%. TTM net income: 17.98B.
- **Margins**: Operating income and net income expanded in absolute terms. TTM operating income: 21.86B. Reported net margin (TTM) implied by TTM revenue/net income = ~53.9%. Operating margin implied = ~65.6%.
- **Cash-Flow**: **No cash flow statement data available** in the supplied evidence.
- **Balance Sheet**: Total assets grew from 81.81B to 91.41B. Total equity grew from 35.99B to 39.73B. Total liabilities grew from 45.82B to 51.68B. Debt-to-equity ratio (Total Liabilities / Total Equity) = 1.30x (2023-12-31).

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality (Quantitative Proxy)**: Net margin expanded from ~50.3% (2023-04-03) to a sustained 53.9% (2024-01-24 onward). Return on equity (ROE) expanded from 41.1% to 45.3%. This indicates improving profitability metrics without available cash flow dissection.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Equity base is large and grew. Leverage ratio (D/E) of 1.30x is moderate. The absence of quarterly debt maturity, liquidity, or interest coverage data limits a full assessment.

**3. Valuation Versus Supplied History**
- **Provided Valuation Ratios**: Net margin (0.539) and ROE (0.453) are at the high end of their 365-day windows (Net Margin: 0.5-0.5, ROE: 0.4-0.5).
- **Unavailable Metrics**: P/E, EV/EBIT, FCF Yield, and P/B ratios are **not available** in the supplied data. This critically limits a relative valuation assessment.

**4. Price Level & Quarterly/Annual Return Context**
- **Price Level**: 279.08 (2024-03-28). 52-week high: 290.96, low: 216.14. Price is near the upper end of its range.
- **Returns**: Quarterly delta (last ~63 days): +7.8%. Annual delta (last ~252 days): +26.7%. Quarterly path shows deceleration: +12.8% (Q2 2023) → +0.7% (Q3) → +12.8% (Q4) → +7.8% (Q1 2024).

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
- **Upside**: Consistent top-line growth (8Q CAGR ~3.5% Q/Q, 8.8% YoY) coupled with expanding margins and a strengthening earnings profile (ROE 45.3%, net margin 53.9%). Equity base is growing.
- **Downside**: **Complete lack of cash-flow data** precludes assessment of cash conversion, capex, or shareholder returns. Price appreciation of +26.7% over one year has brought the stock near its 52-week high (279.08 vs 290.96). Valuation metrics (P/E) are unquantifiable from the supplied data.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
Based strictly on available metrics, the fundamental trajectory is strong: revenue and earnings are growing with expanding profitability and a stable balance sheet. However, the assessment is materially hampered by the absence of cash-flow and key valuation ratios (P/E, FCF Yield). The price has appreciated significantly, trading near its period high. **Quantitative conclusion is conditional**: The fundamental path supports quality, but without cash-flow verification and a valuation anchor, the risk/reward cannot be fully quantified. The stock trades on momentum, but the lack of valuation data makes it impossible to call it cheap or expensive on a point-in-time basis.
V.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Report: Visa Inc. (V)**
**Decision Date: 2024-04-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read**
Visa operates a global digital payments network, facilitating transactions among consumers, merchants, financial institutions, and governments across more than 200 countries. Its core business is a "four-party" model, expanded to include fintechs and digital wallets, where it earns revenue from service, data processing, and international transaction fees, net of client incentives. Visa does not issue cards, extend credit, or bear credit risk. Its strategy is built on three growth pillars: accelerating consumer payments (credit, debit, prepaid), expanding new flows (e.g., Visa Direct for P2P, B2C, B2B payments), and growing value-added services (e.g., issuing solutions, risk and identity, advisory). The company fortifies this with a "network of networks" strategy, strong technology platforms, security, and brand.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated)**
*   **Corporate Action / Regulation (2024-03-26 to 2024-03-28):** Visa and Mastercard announced a landmark $30 billion settlement with U.S. merchants, ending two decades of antitrust litigation over swipe fees. The deal lowers credit card interchange fees by 4 basis points in the U.S. and caps swipe fees until 2030. It also removes anti-steering provisions, allowing merchants to price differently based on card type.
*   **Product / Technology (2024-03-28):** Visa introduced three new AI-backed solutions to enhance its value-added services suite, specifically aimed at improvingizingifying fraud and enhancing payment security.
 securityizing sophisticated forms of fraud.
*   **Market Narrative (2024-03-28 to 024-0):**):24-03-03-31****3-33-03-024-03-31):**024-03-31):**24-03-03-03-03-03-03-03-03-03-03-03-03-03-03-19):** Analyst commentary highlighted Visa's strong economic moat, robust consumer spending, and potential for rapid expansion into regions like the Middle East as key growth catalysts.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Revenue Pressure from Settlement.** The 2024-03-26 settlement directly lowers interchange fee rates and caps them, which could pressure Visa's service revenues, which are based on payment volume. The removal of anti-steering provisions may also lead to merchant steering of consumers toward lower-cost payment methods.
    *   **Offset:** The settlement removes a major, long-standing legal overhang and provides fee stability through 2030. Visa's business model is not directly dependent on interchange fees, and its revenue is tied to transaction volume, which may benefit from a more stable merchant environment.
*   **Risk: Regulatory and Competitive Scrutiny.** The settlement (2024-03-26) follows ongoing regulatory attention, as noted in a 2024-03-11 report that Visa and Mastercard lost a bid to end parts of related swipe fee litigation. The Capital One-Discover merger (mentioned 2024-03-28) could also increase competitive pressure in the payments network space.
    *   **Offset:** Visa's 10-K (2023-11-15) notes a "modest impact" from existing regulations like Regulation II and no significant changes in volume routing. Its vast scale, global network, and brand strength provide a durable competitive moat.
*   **Risk: Dependence on Economic and Consumer Health.** Growth is linked to consumer spending and the migration from cash. A slowdown in the economy or consumer spending could impact transaction volumes.
    *   **Offset:** Recent commentary (2024-02-13, 2024-03-19) points to positive U.S. economic indicators (declining inflation, robust employment) and a secular trend away from cash as ongoing tailwinds.

**4. Qualitative Assessment**
**Positive.** Visa maintains a dominant global franchise with a powerful network effect and a clear strategic focus on high-growth areas like new flows and value-added services. The recent settlement, while imposing near-term fee constraints, resolves a major long-term legal risk. The company is actively investing in technology (AI, security) and benefits from favorable secular trends in digital payments. The primary risks are regulatory and competitive, but the strength of the core business and strategic positioning provide a significant buffer.

ITEM 1. Business
The Walt Disney Company, together with its subsidiaries, is a diversified worldwide entertainment company with operations in three segments: Entertainment, Sports and Experiences.
The terms "Company", "we", "our" and "us" are used in this report to refer collectively to the parent company and the subsidiaries through which businesses are conducted.
Human Capital
The Company's key human capital management objectives are to attract, retain and develop the highest quality talent. To support these objectives, the Company's human resources programs are designed to develop talent to prepare them for critical roles and leadership positions for the future; reward and support employees through competitive pay, benefit and perquisite programs; enhance the Company's culture through efforts aimed at making the workplace more engaging and inclusive; acquire talent and facilitate internal talent mobility to create a high-performing, diverse workforce; engage employees as brand ambassadors of the Company's content, products and experiences; and evolve and invest in technology, tools and resources to enable employees at work.
The Company employed approximately 225,000 people as of September 30, 2023, of which approximately 167,000 were employed in the U.S. and approximately 58,000 were employed outside the U.S. Our global workforce is comprised of approximately 77% full time and 16% part time employees, with another 7% being seasonal employees. A significant number of employees in various parts of our businesses, including employees of our theme parks, and writers, directors, actors and production personnel for our productions are cove

TABLE OF CONTENTS
Content Sales/Licensing and Other
The majority of Content Sales/Licensing revenue is derived from TV/VOD, theatrical and home entertainment distribution. In addition, revenue is generated from music distribution, stage plays and post-production services through Industrial Light & Magic and Skywalker Sound.
The Company also publishes National Geographic magazine, which is reported with Content Sales/Licensing.
TV/VOD Distribution
We license our content to third-party television networks, television stations and other video service providers for distribution to viewers on television or a variety of internet-connected devices, including through other DTC services.
Theatrical Distribution
The Company licenses full-length live-action and animated films to theaters globally. Cumulatively through September 30, 2023, the Company has released approximately 1,100 full-length live-action films and 100 full-length animated films. In the domestic and most major international markets, we generally distribute and market our films directly. In certain international markets our films are distributed by independent companies. In some territories, certain films may be exclusively distributed on our DTC streaming services. During fiscal 2024, we expect to release approximately 15 films, although the ultimate number of releases will depend on when productions resume following the writers/actors' work stoppages.
The Company incurs significant marketing and advertising costs before and throughout the theatrical release of a film in an effort to generate public awareness of the film, to increase the public's intent to view the film and to help generate consumer interest in the subsequent home entertainment and other ancillary markets. These costs are expensed as incurred, which may result in a loss on a film in the theatrical markets, including in periods prior to the theatrical release of the film.
Home Entertainment Distribution
We distribute the Company's film and episodic content in home entertainment markets on DVD and Blu-ray disc, through electronic home video licenses and VOD rentals globally.
Domestically and internationally, we distribute directly to retailers and through independent distribution companies. Electronic formats of our film and episodic content may be purchased through e-tailers such as Apple and Amazon, and MVPDs, such as Comcast and DirecTV, and physical formats are generally sold to retailers, such as Walmart and Target. The Company also operates Disney Movie Club, which sells DVD/Blu-ray discs directly to consumers in the U.S. and Canada.
Distribution of film content in the home entertainment window generally starts within three months after the theatrical release. Electronic formats are typically available approximately four to eight weeks ahead of the physical release. We also license titles to VOD e-tailers concurrent with physical home entertainment distribution.
Distribution of episodic content in the home entertainment window includes electronic sales of season passes that can be purchased prior to, during and after the broadcast season with individual episodes typically available to season pass customers shortly after the initial airing of the show in each territory. Access to individual episodes is also available for electronic purchase shortly after the initial airing in each territory.
Disney Theatrical Group
Disney Theatrical Group develops, produces and licenses live entertainment events on Broadway and around the world. Productions include The Lion King, Frozen, Aladdin and Beauty and the Beast.
Disney Theatrical Group also licenses the Company's IP to Feld Entertainment, the producer of Disney On Ice and Marvel Universe Live!.
Disney Music Group
The Disney Music Group encompasses all aspects of the Company's music commercialization and marketing including: recorded music (Walt Disney Records and Hollywood Records); music publishing; and concerts. Disney Music Group distributes music both physically and digitally and also licenses music throughout the world in various forms of media, including: television; print; gaming; and consumer products.
Equity Investment
The Company has a 30% effective interest in Tata Play Limited, which operates a direct-to-home satellite distribution platform in India.
Content Production and Acquisition
Produced content primarily consists of original films and episodic programs, network news and daytime/nighttime content and licensed content includes acquired episodic programming rights. Original content is generally produced under the following banners: ABC Signature; Disney Branded Television; FX Productions; Lucasfilm; Marvel; National Geographic Studios; Pixar;

Costs to produce content are generally capitalized and allocated across Entertainment's businesses based on the estimated relative value of the distribution windows.
Generally, the Company has full production and distribution rights to its IP. However, prior to the Company's acquisition of Marvel, Sony Pictures Entertainment licensed from Marvel the rights to produce and distribute Spider-Man films in all windows except for the merchandise rights, which the Company retains.

Our television and radio stations primarily compete for audiences and advertisers in local market areas.
Linear Networks compete with other networks for carriage by MVPDs. The Company's contractual agreements with MVPDs are renewed or renegotiated from time to time in the ordinary course of business. **Quantitative Report: DIS (Decision Date: 2024-04-01)**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
*   **Revenue & Earnings:** TTM revenue is $88.94B. The latest quarter (2023-12-30) shows revenue of $23.55B, a +0.2% YoY increase from $23.51B. Quarterly EPS is volatile: $1.04 (Q1 2024), $0.15 (Q4 2023), -$0.25 (Q3 2023), $0.69 (Q2 2023). TTM net income is $4.18B.
*   **Margins:** Latest operating margin is 15.4% (operating income $3.88B on revenue $23.55B). Latest net margin is 4.7%.
*   **Cash-Flow:** No quarterly or TTM cash flow data is supplied.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Total equity has grown from $94.66B (2022-04-02) to $105.50B (2023-12-30). Total liabilities decreased from $107.79B to $92.27B over the same period. Total debt was $46.43B as of 2023-09-30.

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** Earnings are highly volatile on a quarterly basis, with a recent loss in Q3 2023 (EPS -$0.25). The YoY EPS growth of +48.6% in the latest quarter is driven by a low base (Q1 2023 EPS $0.70). The TTM net margin of 4.7% is low.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** The equity base is large ($105.50B) and growing. The debt level of $46.43B (as of 2023-09-30) is significant but appears stable. The ratio of total liabilities to total equity improved from 1.14x (2022-04-02) to 0.87x (2023-12-30).

**3. Valuation versus Supplied History**
*   The latest P/E (diluted) is 53.7x, within the 1-year range of 33.9x to 77.0x. It is above the 1-year median of ~53.1x (based on sparse history).
*   The latest P/B ratio is 2.13x, at the top of its 1-year range of 1.4x to 2.1x.
*   The earnings yield is 1.9% (inverse of P/E).
*   The latest ROE is 4.0%, within its 1-year range of 0.0 to 0.0 (note: range data appears truncated).

**4. Price Level and Return Context versus Fundamentals**
*   **Price Level:** The stock closed at $122.36 on 2024-03-28, near its 1-year high of $123.74.
*   **Returns:** The quarterly return (from 2023-12-27) is +35.4%. The annual return (from 2023-03-28) is +29.0%.
*   **Context:** The strong recent price momentum (+35.4% in one quarter) has occurred alongside a modest YoY revenue increase (+0.2%) and a volatile earnings trajectory. The stock's valuation (P/E 53.7x, P/B 2.13x) is at the higher end of its recent history.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**
*   **Upside:** Continued improvement in operating margin (currently 15.4%) and stabilization of quarterly earnings could justify the current valuation. The strengthening balance sheet (rising liabilities, growing equity) is a positive fundamental trend.
*   **Downside:** The high valuation (P/E 53.7x) leaves little margin for error. A reversion of quarterly earnings volatility (e.g., another loss quarter) or a failure to grow revenue could lead to a significant de-rating. The lack of supplied cash flow data is a gap in assessing true financial health.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The quantitative profile presents a tension. The balance sheet is resilient and improving, and the latest quarter showed strong earnings growth YoY. However, this is set against a backdrop of high quarterly earnings volatility, a low net margin (4.7%), and a stock price that has surged 35.4% in the last quarter, pushing valuation multiples (P/E 53.7x, P/B 2.13x) to the upper end of their recent ranges. The risk is that the current price already reflects a significant improvement in fundamentals that has yet to be consistently demonstrated in the earnings trajectory. The reward depends on the durability of the latest quarter's performance. The quantitative risk/reward appears unfavorable at this price level and valuation, given the supplied evidence of earnings volatility and the absence of cash flow data.
DIS.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: The Walt Disney Company (DIS)**  
**Decision Date:** 2024-04-01 | **Evidence Cutoff:** Pre-2024-04-01  

---

### 1. Business/Franchise Read  
The Walt Disney Company is a diversified worldwide entertainment company operating through three segments: **Entertainment**, **Sports**, and **Experiences**.  

- **Entertainment**: Encompasses global film/TV production, linear networks (ABC, Disney Channels, FX, National Geographic), direct-to-consumer (DTC) streaming (Disney+, Hulu), and content licensing/sales.  
  - Disney+ Core (global) had ~113 million paid subscribers as of September 30, 2023.  
  - Hulu (U.S.) had ~49 million paid subscribers as of the same date.  
  - The company owns a vast content library (~5,100 live-action films, 400 animated films, numerous series).  
- **Sports**: Includes ESPN and sports-related content distribution.  
- **Experiences**: Covers theme parks, resorts, cruise lines, and consumer products.  

The company employs ~225,000 people and emphasizes human capital initiatives (e.g., Disney Aspire education program) and environmental sustainability goals for 2030.  

---

### 2. Recent Developments (Dated)  
**Stock & Market Sentiment:**  
- 2024-03-29: Disney highlighted as a "trending stock" on Zacks.com, indicating heightened retail investor attention.  
- 2024-03-27: Disney stock hit a 52-week high; one analyst projected 15% upside based on anticipated free cash flow growth.  
- 2024-03-26: Disney stock rose while the broader market dipped, demonstrating relative strength.  

**Streaming & Competitive Positioning:**  
- 2024-03-27: Article argues Disney is "the better buy in streaming today" versus Netflix, citing its sports content as a "huge advantage."  
- 2024-03-27: Disney exploring ad business growth through partnerships with "a trillion-dollar company" and "an unstoppable growth stock" (specific partners not named in the text).  

**Corporate & Legal Actions:**  
- 2024-03-28: Disney settled its legal dispute with Florida Governor DeSantis (details not elaborated).  
- 2024-03-27: Media industry veteran criticized the Disney board for being "too deferential" to CEO Bob Iger, specifically citing the 21st Century Fox acquisition as a poor decision.  
- 2024-03-26: Disney faced a "rival theme park operator and activist investors" (specific activists not named in the text).  

**Regulatory & Political Environment:**  
- 2024-02-27: Disney filed an appeal after a federal judge dismissed its lawsuit against the Florida government, arguing the ruling could "set a dangerous precedent" for punishing political speech.  
- 2024-02-15: Disney sued the Florida government for First Amendment violations.  
- 2024-02-15: A separate lawsuit alleged Disney engaged in "illegal discrimination" via casting quotas (Disney’s response not provided in the text).  

---

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks & Offsets  
**Risks:**  
- **Legal & Political Exposure**: Ongoing litigation with the Florida government creates uncertainty and potential reputational damage. The stalemate in the DeSantis battle (2024-03-29) may prolong operational distractions in a key market.  
- **Activist Investor Pressure**: The presence of activist investors (2024-03-26) could lead to pressure on strategy, capital allocation, or management.  
- **Content Strategy & Acquisition Scrutiny**: The 21st Century Fox acquisition remains a point of criticism (2024-03-27), suggesting investor skepticism about past strategic decisions.  
- **Regulatory & Social Risk**: Lawsuits alleging discrimination (2024-02-15) and political controversies (2024-03-22 shareholder letter) could impact brand perception and consumer sentiment.  

**Offsets:**  
- **Streaming Competitive Strength**: Disney’s sports content provides a distinct advantage in the streaming wars (2024-03-27).  
- **Financial Narrative**: Analyst views highlight undervaluation and potential free cash flow expansion (2024-03-27).  
- **Legal Settlement**: The resolution of the DeSantis fight (2024-03-28) removes one layer of immediate conflict, though appeals continue.  
- **Diversified Business Model**: Profitability across all parks (cited in web context, 2024-02-27) and a broad content library provide foundational stability.  

---

### 4. Qualitative Assessment  
**Neutral-to-Slightly-Positive**.  

**Rationale**:  
- **Positive Catalysts**: Disney’s streaming narrative is improving, with analyst upgrades and competitive positioning in sports. Cost discipline and free cash flow potential are highlighted. The stock’s recent strength indicates renewed market confidence.  
- **Significant Headwinds**: Ongoing legal battles with Florida and potential activist interventions create overhang. Past strategic missteps (Fox acquisition) remain a concern. The company must navigate these while sustaining streaming profitability and park performance.  
- **Overall**: The evidence points to a company with strong assets and improving sentiment, but material risks from external conflicts and governance debates temper the outlook. The balance of catalysts and risks suggests limited near-term upside until legal clarity improves.  

---  
*Report based solely on supplied point-in-time evidence. No inferred financial data or multiples used.*

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Disney's improving balance sheet and streaming narrative are offset by a stretched valuation (P/E 53.7x, P/B at 1-year high) after a 35% quarterly surge, combined with persistent earnings volatility and ongoing legal/political overhangs. The risk/reward is roughly balanced at current levels.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Disney's improving balance sheet and streaming narrative are offset by a stretched valuation (P/E 53.7x, P/B at 1-year high) after a 35% quarterly surge, combined with persistent earnings volatility and ongoing legal/political overhangs. The risk/reward is roughly balanced at current levels.
key_factors: P/E of 53.7x and P/B of 2.13x are at the upper end of their 1-year ranges following a +35.4% quarterly price surge, leaving little margin for error on earnings.
Quarterly EPS is highly volatile ($1.04, $0.15, -$0.25, $0.69 over the last four quarters) with a low TTM net margin of 4.7%, making the premium valuation hard to sustain.
Balance sheet is strengthening with total liabilities-to-equity improving from 1.14x to 0.87x and equity growing to $105.5B, providing fundamental support.

Ongoing legal battles with Florida (appeal filed 2024-02-27) and activist investor pressure (2024-03-26) create material overhang, partially offset by the DeSantis settlement (2024-03-28) and streaming competitive advantages in sports content.

Latest 10-K business section (newest first):
  filing_date=2023-08-04 section=business period_end=2023-06-30
Item 1. Business.
The Procter & Gamble Company (the Company) is focused on providing branded products of superior quality and value to improve the lives of the world's consumers, now and for generations to come. The Company was incorporated in Ohio in 1905, having first been established as a New Jersey corporation in 1890, and was built from a business founded in Cincinnati in 1837 by William Procter and James Gamble.
Additional information required by this item is incorporated herein by reference to Management's Discussion and Analysis (MD&A); and Notes 1 and 2 to our Consolidated Financial Statements. Unless the context indicates otherwise, the terms "Company," "P&G," "we," "our" or "us" as used herein refer to The Procter & Gamble Company (the registrant) and its subsidiaries. Throughout this Form 10-K, we incorporate by reference information from other documents filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).
The Company's Annual Report on Form 10-K, quarterly reports on Form 10-Q and current reports on Form 8-K, and amendments thereto, are filed electronically with the SEC. The SEC maintains an internet site that contains these reports at: www.sec.gov. Reports can also be accessed through links from our website at: www.pginvestor.com. P&G includes the website link solely as a textual reference and the information on our website is not incorporated by reference into this report.
Copies of these reports are also available, without charge, by contacting EQ Shareowner Services, 1100 Centre Pointe Curve, Suite 101, Mendota, MN 55120-4100.
Financial Information about Segments
Information about our reportable segments can be found in the MD&A and Note 2 to our Consolidated Financial Statements.
Narrative Description of Business
Business Model
. Our business model is built to deliver balanced top- and bottom-line growth and value creation. We rely on 
…[truncated 3692 chars]…
ten holding a leadership or significant market share position. We support our products with advertising, promotions and other marketing vehicles to build awareness and trial of our brands and products in conjunction with our sales force. We believe this combination provides the
2        The Procter & Gamble Company
most efficient method of marketing for these types of products. Product quality, performance, value and packaging are also important differentiating factors.
Government Regulation
. Our Company is subject to a wide variety of laws and regulations across the countries in which we do business. In the United States, many of our products and manufacturing operations are subject to one or more federal or state regulatory agencies, including the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA), the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) and the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC). We are also subject to anti-corruption laws and regulations, such as the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, and antitrust and competition laws and regulations that govern our dealings with suppliers, customers, competitors and government officials.
In addition, many foreign jurisdictions in which we do business have regulations and regulatory bodies that govern similar aspects of our operations and products, in some cases to an even more significant degree. We are also subject to expanding laws and regulations related to environmental protection and other sustainability-related matters, non-financial reporting and diligence, labor and employment, trade, taxation and data privacy and protection, including the European Union's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and similar regulations in states within the United States and in countries around the world.
The Company has in place compliance programs and internal and external experts to help guide our business in complying with these and other existing laws and regulations that apply to us around the globe; and we have made, and plan to continue making, necessary expenditures for compliance with these laws and regulations. We also expect that our many suppliers, consultants and other third parties working on our behalf share our commitment to compliance, and we have policies and procedures in place to manage these relationships, though they inherently involve a lesser degree of control over operations and governance. We do not expect that the Company's expenditures for compliance with current government regulations, including current environmental regulations, will have a material effect on our total capital expenditures, earnings or competitive position in fiscal year 2024 as compared to prior periods.
Human Capital
. Our employees are a key source of competitive advantage. Their actions, guided by our Purpose, Values and Principles (PVPs), are critical to the long-term success of our business. We aim to retain our talented employees by offering competitive compensation and benefits, strong career development and a respectful and inclusive culture that provides equal opportunity for all.
Our Board of Directors, through the Compensation and Leadership Development Committee (C&LD Committee), provides oversight of the Company's policies and strategy relating to talent including diversity, equality and inclusion as well as the Company's compensation principles and practices. The C&LD Committee also evaluates and approves the Company's compensation plans, policies and programs applicable to our senior executives.
Employees
As of June 30, 2023, the Company had approximately 107,000 employees, an increase of 1% versus the prior year due primarily to business growth. The total number of employees is an estimate of total Company employees excluding interns, co-ops, contractors and employees of joint ventures. 49% of our employees are in manufacturing roles and 27% of our employees are located in the United States. 41% of our global employees are women and 30% of our U.S. employees identify as multicultural.
Training and Development
We focus on attracting, developing and retaining skilled and diverse talent, both from universities and the broader market. We recruit from among the best universities across markets in which we compete and are generally able to select from the top talent. We focus on developing our employees by providing a variety of job experiences, training programs and skill development opportunities. Given our develop-from-within model for staffing most of our senior leadership positions, it is particularly important for us to ensure holistic growth and full engagement of our employees.
Diversity, Equality and Inclusion
As a consumer products company, we believe that it is important for our workforce to reflect the diversity of our consumers worldwide. We also seek to foster an inclusive work environment where each individual can bring their authentic self, which helps drive innovation and enables us to better serve our consumers. We aspire to achieve equal gender representation globally and at key management and leadership levels. Within the U.S. workforce, our aspiration is to achieve 40% multicultural representation overall as well as at management and leadership levels.
Compensation and Benefits
Market-competitive compensation and reward programs are critical elements of our employee value equation to attract and retain the best talent. Our total rewards programs are based on the principles of paying for performance, paying competitively versus peer companies that we compete with for talent in the marketplace and focusing on long-term success through a combination of short-term and long-term incentive programs. We also offer competitive benefit programs, including retirement plans and health insurance in line with local country practices with flexibility to accommodate the needs of a diverse workforce.
Sustainability
. Environmental sustainability is integrated into our business strategy to offer consumers irresistibly superior products that are more sustainable. Our aim is to deliver balanced top- and bottom-line growth, value creation and key sustainability objectives. In 2021, the Company announced a 2040 net zero ambition and published a Climate Transition Action
The Procter & Gamble Company        3
Plan, which describes the Company's ongoing efforts toward reducing greenhouse gas emissions across scopes 1 and 2 and elements of scope 3. This includes a long-term objective of net zero emissions for scopes 1 and 2, elements of scope 3 and interim goals to help us pace our progress. The Company has also declared goals towards using renewable electricity for our operations, reducing use of virgin petroleum-based plastic in packaging, increasing the recyclability or reusability of packaging, responsible sourcing of key forest-based commodities, improving efficiency of water usage in our operations and driving a global portfolio of water restoration projects to address water scarcity.
We use the standards and guidelines of the Global Reporting Initiative, Sustainability Accounting Standards Board (SASB) industry specific standards and the Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures (TCFD) to inform our sustainability and related disclosures included in this Annual Report, our Proxy Statement and our sustainability reports. The "materiality" thresholds in those standards and guidelines may differ from the concept of "materiality" for purposes of the federal securities laws and disclosures required by the Commission's rules in this Annual Report. References to our sustainability reports and website are for informational purposes only and neither the sustainability reports nor the other information on our website is incorporated by reference into this Annual Report on Form 10-K. Additional detailed information on our sustainability efforts can be found on our website at https://pginvestor.com/esg.
PG.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: PG (Procter & Gamble)**
**Decision Date: 2024-04-01 | Evidence Cutoff: 2024-03-28**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
*   **Revenue & Earnings:** Latest quarterly revenue (2023-12-31) was $21.44B, a QoQ decline of -2.0% but a YoY increase of +3.2%. TTM revenue is $83.93B. Latest quarterly EPS diluted was $1.40, a sharp QoQ decline of -23.5% and a YoY decline of -11.9%. TTM net income is $14.87B.
*   **Margins:** Latest gross margin was 52.7% ($11.30B / $21.44B). Latest operating margin was 20.7% ($4.43B / $21.44B). Latest net margin was 16.3% ($3.49B / $21.44B). The 365-day average net margin was 0.177 (17.7%).
*   **Cash-Flow:** No quarterly or TTM free cash flow data is available in the supplied evidence.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Total equity has grown from $44.73B (2022-12-31) to $48.83B (2023-12-31). The debt-to-equity ratio (total liabilities / total equity) was 1.47x ($71.88B / $48.83B) as of 2023-12-31.

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** The latest quarter shows a disconnect: revenue grew YoY (+3.2%) while EPS diluted fell (-11.9%), indicating margin compression. Gross profit grew YoY (+14.4%), but operating income declined, suggesting rising operating costs. The absence of cash flow data prevents assessment of earnings-to-cash conversion.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** The equity base is stable and growing. The leverage ratio of 1.47x is elevated but has been consistent over the provided period (range: 1.47x to 1.64x).

**3. Valuation versus Company's Own Supplied History**
*   **Current Valuation (2024-03-28):** PE diluted = 26.94, Price/Book = 8.20, Price/Sales = 4.77, Earnings Yield = 3.7%.
*   **Historical Context (365-day window):** The PE diluted has ranged from 23.2 to 27.0. The current PE of 26.94 is at the very top of this range. The Price/Book ratio of 8.20 is within its 7.4 to 8.5 range. The net margin of 0.177 and ROE of 0.304 are at the lower end of their respective 365-day ranges (0.2-0.2 and 0.3-0.3).

**4. Price Level and Quarterly/Annual Return Context**
*   **Price Level:** The latest close (2024-03-28) was $162.25, near the 365-day high of $163.14.
*   **Returns:** The stock has returned +11.1% over the last quarter (~63 trading days) and +10.9% over the last year (~252 trading days). The quarterly path shows a recent strong rally after a period of flat-to-negative returns.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**
*   **Upside:** Continued revenue growth (TTM $83.93B) and a stable/growing equity base ($48.83B) provide a fundamental floor. A reversion of margins toward the 365-day average net margin of 17.7% from the latest 16.3% could support earnings.
*   **Downside:** The valuation is stretched at a 26.94x PE, at the top of its recent range, while the latest earnings trajectory is negative (EPS -11.9% YoY). A failure to stabilize margins or a contraction in the elevated PE multiple presents clear downside risk.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The quantitative profile presents a tension. The stock has appreciated significantly (+11.1% quarterly) to a valuation (PE 26.94) at the top of its recent range, while the most recent fundamental data shows deteriorating earnings (EPS -11.9% YoY) and compressed margins. The balance sheet is resilient, but the absence of cash flow data is a gap. The risk/reward appears skewed to the downside, as the price has moved ahead of the near-term earnings trajectory, leaving the stock vulnerable to a valuation correction if margin pressure persists.
PG.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment: Procter & Gamble (PG)**
**Decision Date:** 2024-04-01

### 1. Business/Franchise Read
Based on the supplied 10-K business description (filing_date=2023-08-04), Procter & Gamble is a global consumer staples leader with a portfolio of branded products sold in approximately 180 countries. The business model is built on delivering balanced top-line and bottom-line growth through innovation and superiority across product performance, packaging, communication, retail execution, and value. The company operates in highly competitive markets, competing with both other global brands and retailers' private-label brands. Key structural attributes include a wide product portfolio, a broad distribution network including mass merchandisers and e-commerce, and significant manufacturing and R&D capabilities. A key dependency is noted, with Walmart Inc. representing approximately 15% of total sales.

### 2. Recent Narrative Developments
*   **Growth and Financials:** The narrative indicates organic sales grew 4% year-over-year in the most recent reported quarter (as of 2024-02-21, 2024-03-20). One source (2024-03-25) notes the company raised its core EPS growth guidance following strong second-quarter results.
*   **Competitive Environment & Consumer Trends:** A specific headwind was identified on 2024-03-22: Procter & Gamble is facing short-term pressure from a decrease in product volume as consumers shift toward store-branded (private-label) alternatives.
*   **Corporate Actions & Capital Allocation:** Multiple articles (2024-03-28, 2024-03-30) highlight PG's status as a stable dividend stock, with an expectation of a dividend increase in April. The company is framed as a source of stability and passive income rather than high growth.
*   **Legal & Regulatory:** The company faces ongoing litigation. A lawsuit concerning discriminatory employment practices was filed on 2024-02-12. Conversely, the company successfully defeated class-action claims in a different case on 2024-03-25, and another false-advertising suit was voluntarily dismissed on 2024-03-04.

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
*   **Risks:**
    *   **Volume Growth & Private Label Competition:** Confirmed in news (2024-03-22), a near-term risk is consumer downshifting to store brands, which pressures volume growth for branded products.
    *   **Customer Concentration:** The 10-K notes sales to Walmart represent ~15% of total sales, creating dependency on a single large retailer.
    *   **Legal and Regulatory Exposure:** The company is subject to a wide range of global regulations (per 10-K) and active litigation (as per 2024-02-12 and 2024-03-11 sources), posing reputational and financial risks.
    *   **Input Cost Fluctuation:** The 10-K states raw material and commodity prices are subject to change and may not always be passed to customers.
*   **Offsets:**
    *   **Brand Strength and Superiority Strategy:** The core business model, as described in the 10-K, is explicitly designed to compete against private labels through continuous product and marketing superiority.
    *   **Scale and Operational Focus:** Global scale, a wide portfolio, and a stated focus on productivity improvement provide a buffer against competition and input cost volatility.
    *   **Legal Resolution Capability:** Recent news shows the company successfully defending against or resolving certain legal claims (2024-03-04, 2024-03-25).

### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral.**
The franchise is stable and well-established, with a strategy explicitly designed to combat private-label competition. The company maintains financial discipline, as evidenced by recent guidance raises and consistent dividend expectations. However, the current narrative is conflicted: positive guidance and dividend strength are counterbalanced by clear evidence of near-term volume headwinds from consumer trading down. The risk from private-label competition is currently active and notable. The assessment is therefore neutral, reflecting a strong, defensive business facing a confirmed near-term operational challenge.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: PG is a high-quality defensive franchise with stable fundamentals, but the stock is trading at a stretched valuation (PE 26.94, at the top of its 365-day range) while near-term earnings are deteriorating (EPS -11.9% YoY) and volume headwinds from private-label competition are confirmed. The risk/reward is balanced at current levels.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: PG is a high-quality defensive franchise with stable fundamentals, but the stock is trading at a stretched valuation (PE 26.94, at the top of its 365-day range) while near-term earnings are deteriorating (EPS -11.9% YoY) and volume headwinds from private-label competition are confirmed. The risk/reward is balanced at current levels.
key_factors: Valuation stretched: PE diluted of 26.94 as of 2024-03-28 sits at the very top of its 365-day range (23.2–27.0), leaving limited upside and vulnerability to multiple compression.
Earnings deterioration: Latest quarterly EPS diluted fell 11.9% YoY despite 3.2% revenue growth, indicating margin compression; net margin of 16.3% is below the 365-day average of 17.7%.
Confirmed volume headwind: News from 2024-03-22 reports consumers shifting toward private-label/store-branded alternatives, pressuring product volumes for PG's branded portfolio.

Defensive franchise strength: Organic sales grew 4% YoY in the most recent quarter, the company raised core EPS growth guidance (2024-03-25), and the balance sheet shows growing equity ($48.83B) with consistent leverage (1.47x D/E).

ITEM 1. BUSINESS
THE COMPANY
Dow Inc. was incorporated on August 30, 2018, under Delaware law, to serve as a holding company for The Dow Chemical Company and its consolidated subsidiaries ("TDCC" and together with Dow Inc., "Dow" or the "Company"). Dow Inc. operates all of its businesses through TDCC, a wholly owned subsidiary, which was incorporated in 1947 under Delaware law and is the successor to a Michigan corporation, of the same name, organized in 1897. The Company's principal executive offices are located at 2211 H.H. Dow Way, Midland, Michigan 48674.
Available Information
The Company's Annual Reports on Form 10-K, Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q and Current Reports on Form 8-K, and amendments to those reports filed or furnished pursuant to Section 13(a) or 15(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, are available free of charge at www.dow.com/investors, as soon as reasonably practicable after the reports are electronically filed or furnished with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC"). The SEC maintains a website that contains these reports as well as proxy statements and other information regarding issuers that file electronically. The SEC's website is www.sec.gov. Dow's website and its content are not deemed incorporated by reference into this report.
